prose:
Fukui reactivity descriptors were computed on benzaldehyde using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, using PySCF. The calculation performed single-point Fukui analysis on the user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz geometry (not relaxed), computing condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and the dual descriptor (Δf = f⁺ − f⁻) per atom via finite-difference of Mulliken charges between the neutral, singly-ionized cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states. All three SCF calculations converged cleanly. The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (ortho-carbon on the benzene ring), with f⁺ = 0.3994; the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen), with f⁻ = 0.2384. Charge conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = sum f⁻ = 1.0000). The results indicate that electrophilic aromatic substitution would favor ortho-attack, consistent with the electron-withdrawing aldehyde group, while nucleophilic attack targets the carbonyl oxygen.</prose>
